Linux System Updates and Automation

  1. Why should a system administrator review the list of packages after apt update is run?
    • After completing the “apt update” command a system administrator should check the list of packages to see which software will be upgraded and identify any unexpected or critical changes. This will help ensure that important services are not unintentionally modified before installing the updates.
  2. As a Linux system administrator managing patching, what is a key risk to evaluate during implementation and a single strategy to mitigate it?
    • One big risk involved with implementing patches is that they can cause current application/services to fail or function improperly because of incompatibility problems. One common method used to mitigate this type of risk is to test patches in a staging/test environment prior to deployment to production environments.
